document.title: R.A.T.E. — Reinforcement-based Assessment of Target Elements: Factor Assessment System (FAS)
document.subtitle: A Framework for Evaluating Influential Variables in Predictive Models

project.type: Data Analytics and Factor Intelligence Platform
project.purpose: To identify, rank, analyze, and interpret the most influential factors in predictive models across multiple domains. Sits between raw datasets and predictive model building — not only predicting outcomes but explaining prediction inputs through systematic factor assessment.

background_and_motivation.core_problem: Predictive systems depend heavily on the quality and relevance of input factors. Identifying truly influential variables is difficult but essential in predictive analytics.
background_and_motivation.solution_approach: A systematic factor assessment framework using statistical, ML, and reinforcement-oriented methods.

objectives: Analyze datasets from different predictive domains and detect relevant influencing variables.
Rank factors using statistical and ML techniques: correlation analysis, regression analysis, feature importance, decision trees, and random forest ranking.
Introduce reinforcement-oriented mechanisms for error-sensitive refinement and novelty-case review.
Improve prediction quality, model transparency, and decision support through better factor selection and interpretation.
Generate visual and exportable outputs for academic, research, and analytical use.
intended_audience: Analysts or researchers using the platform for factor assessment

glossary.RL: Reinforcement Learning / Reinforcement-oriented logic in this project context
glossary.Feature_Factor: An input variable influencing model predictions
glossary.Novelty_Case: An unusual or previously unseen case requiring manual reinforcement or review
glossary.Factor_Ranking: Ordering features based on their contribution to prediction outcomes

references: Bishop — Pattern Recognition and Machine Learning
James et al. — An Introduction to Statistical Learning

predictive_evaluation.name: Predictive Evaluation Module
responsibilities: Computing evaluation metrics: accuracy, MSE, RMSE, precision, recall

user_classes.description: Maintains the platform, users, configurations, logs, and storage.
Uploads datasets, configures analysis, runs factor evaluation, views results.
Performs experimental runs, compares algorithms, studies novelty cases.
Views outputs, reports, model results, and interpretation dashboards.

operating_environment.browser_support: Modern browsers (if deployed as web app)
operating_environment.hardware: Standard academic laptops or desktops — no dedicated hardware required for v1

assumptions_and_dependencies: Suitable datasets will be available from Kaggle, UCI, or similar repositories.
Users will know the target variable relevant to their prediction task.
Libraries required for preprocessing, analysis, and modeling will be installed.
The project will initially operate on structured tabular datasets.
Domain examples in the presentation are representative and may be extended later.
design_and_implementation_constraints: The system depends on the availability and quality of external datasets from open repositories or government sources.
Different domains will produce heterogeneous data structures requiring flexible preprocessing.
The RL component is conceptually present in the project title and methodology but the exact RL algorithm is not fully defined — implementation must refine it carefully.
The system should remain understandable to academic evaluators and must not become an over-complex black box.

design_rules: Workflow order must map to methodology: data collection → preprocessing → feature identification → factor assessment → model evaluation → visualization.
Interface shall support tables, filters, plots, and model-result panels.
Interface shall visually distinguish raw features, selected factors, rejected factors, and novelty alerts.
Interface should support academic demonstration with simple navigation.

external_interfaces.hardware_interfaces: No dedicated hardware required for v1. Runs on standard laptops/desktops.
software_interfaces: ML libraries for regression, tree models, and feature importance
Optional RL or rule-based modules for reinforcement/error refinement
external_interfaces.communication_interfaces: HTTP/HTTPS for web deployment. REST-style JSON exchange if external dataset APIs are used.

user_management.description: The system shall support secure login for registered users.
The system shall provide role-based access control for administrators, analysts, researchers, and reviewers.
The system shall store user activity logs for important actions such as upload, analysis execution, novelty review, and report generation.

dataset_upload_and_management.description: The system shall allow users to upload datasets in CSV and Excel formats.
The system shall allow users to label the domain of the dataset as transportation, academic, agriculture, environmental, or custom.
The system shall validate file structure and reject corrupted or unsupported datasets.
The system shall display a dataset preview after upload.
The system shall store dataset metadata: file name, upload time, size, number of rows, number of columns, and selected target variable.

data_preprocessing.description: The system shall support data cleaning operations including duplicate removal and invalid record handling.
The system shall detect missing values and provide configurable strategies for handling them.
The system shall support normalization of numerical data.
The system shall support feature encoding for categorical variables.
The system shall provide a preprocessing summary report showing the transformations applied.
The system shall allow users to rerun preprocessing with modified settings.

feature_identification.description: The system shall allow the user to define the prediction target variable.
The system shall list all candidate input features.
The system shall allow manual inclusion or exclusion of selected factors.
The system shall support domain-specific example factor templates: weather, traffic congestion, operational delays, attendance, study hours, previous grades, rainfall, soil quality, and temperature.
The system shall maintain the selected feature set for each experiment run.

factor_assessment.description: The system shall perform correlation analysis for feature relevance estimation.
The system shall perform regression-based analysis when applicable.
The system shall compute feature importance using machine learning models.
The system shall support decision tree and random forest based feature ranking.
The system shall rank features in descending order of importance.
The system shall display factor scores, rank positions, and analysis method used.
The system shall support comparison of rankings generated by multiple methods.

reinforcement_oriented_error_assessment.description: The system shall include a reinforcement-oriented component for error-sensitive refinement, corresponding to 'Reinforcement Against Errors' in the project presentation.
The system shall track model errors or low-confidence outcomes and use them to trigger reassessment of selected factors.
The system shall allow configurable reward or penalty logic, or equivalent rule-based scoring, to guide refinement of selected features.
The system shall store each reassessment cycle as part of experiment history.
The system should support future replacement of the rule-based refinement engine with a true RL agent if not implemented in the first release.

model_training_and_evaluation.description: The system shall train predictive models using the selected features.
The system shall allow comparison between full-feature models and selected-factor models.
The system shall compute metrics such as accuracy, MSE, RMSE, precision, and recall depending on the prediction problem.
The system shall store metric history for each experiment run.
The system shall identify the best-performing feature set based on user-selected evaluation criteria.

novelty_case_handling.description: The system shall detect or flag potential novelty cases, outliers, or uncertain patterns during evaluation.
The system shall allow manual reinforcement for novelty cases as mentioned in the presentation.
The system shall allow a reviewer to annotate novelty cases with comments and decisions.
The system shall preserve novelty-case decisions for future comparison and improvement cycles.

visualization_and_interpretation.description: The system shall generate feature-importance graphs.
The system shall generate correlation heatmaps.
The system shall generate comparative performance charts.
The system shall provide visual comparison of multiple factor assessment methods.
The system shall support export of plots in image or report-friendly formats.

reporting_and_export.description: The system shall generate downloadable reports summarizing dataset details, preprocessing actions, selected features, factor scores, model metrics, and visual outputs.
The system shall allow export of ranked factor tables in CSV or Excel format.
The system shall store past reports and analysis sessions for later viewing.

performance.description: The system should process typical academic-scale tabular datasets within acceptable time on standard hardware.
Feature ranking and model evaluation should complete within a practical duration suitable for interactive use.
Visualization generation should not block the user interface for excessive time.

reliability.description: The system shall not lose uploaded datasets or experiment metadata during normal operation.
The system shall handle invalid input gracefully and provide meaningful error messages.
The system shall preserve experiment history across application restarts if persistent storage is used.

usability.description: The interface shall be understandable to users with basic knowledge of machine learning.
The workflow shall clearly map to the methodology in the presentation so users can follow the project logic easily.
Output charts and reports shall be readable and suitable for academic presentation.

security.description: User authentication data shall be securely stored.
Only authorized users shall access analysis history and uploaded datasets.
If deployed online, secure communication shall be enabled.

maintainability.description: The software shall use modular design so preprocessing, ranking, evaluation, and visualization components can be modified independently.
The codebase shall be documented sufficiently for student team maintenance.
Configuration-driven design should be used where possible for thresholds, selected algorithms, and novelty rules.

scalability.description: The system should support new prediction domains beyond those listed in the presentation.
The system should support future integration of deeper RL methods and explainability modules.

portability.description: The system should run on Windows and Linux.
If implemented as a web app, it should run on modern browsers.

required_components: Sufficient records for model training and evaluation

UC-01.name: Perform Factor Assessment
UC-01.preconditions: User is logged in and has a valid dataset.
UC-01.postconditions: Results are saved and accessible from history.

UC-02.name: Evaluate Selected Features
main_flow: System compares model results with baseline.
UC-02.postconditions: Evaluation metrics and comparison results stored.

UC-03.name: Review Novelty Case
UC-03.preconditions: A novelty case has been flagged by the system.
main_flow: User inspects factor ranking and model behavior.
User adds comment or corrective action.
UC-03.postconditions: Novelty case decision preserved for future improvement cycles.

database_schema.note: Suggested logical schema for relational storage.

business_rules: The user must select exactly one target variable per experiment.
A dataset must pass validation before preprocessing can begin.
Factor ranking cannot execute until preprocessing and target selection are complete.
Model evaluation cannot execute until at least one factor assessment result exists.
Novelty-case review is mandatory for any case flagged as critical by system rules.
error_handling_requirements: The system shall notify users about unsupported file formats.
The system shall notify users when the target variable is missing or invalid.
The system shall notify users when preprocessing fails.
The system shall notify users when a model cannot be trained due to insufficient data.
The system shall preserve logs for debugging failed experiment runs.
validation_and_acceptance_criteria: System supports dataset upload and preprocessing for at least one dataset from the proposed domains.
System can identify candidate features and compute factor rankings using at least two methods from the presentation.
System trains a predictive model and computes at least the required evaluation metrics relevant to the task.
System supports novelty-case review and stores reviewer decisions.
System generates visualizations including feature-importance graphs, heatmaps, and comparative charts.
System exports a readable report summarizing the full analysis workflow.
risks_and_limitations: Real-world datasets may be noisy, incomplete, or inconsistent across domains.
Different ranking algorithms may disagree on factor importance.
Reinforcement-oriented logic may need careful simplification for a first academic implementation — the presentation names the concept but does not fully specify its algorithm.
Novelty detection quality will depend on threshold design and reviewer consistency.
Multi-domain generalization may be limited in early versions.
future_enhancements: Add a fully defined RL agent for adaptive factor selection.
Add multi-user annotations and collaborative review.
Add domain-specific templates for transportation, education, and agriculture.
Add model recommendation based on dataset type.

requirement_traceability_matrix.presentation_element: Correlation, regression, random forest, feature ranking
